ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Nasi Goreng Pattaya; closest parent 'fried rice food product' (FOODON_00002694) used as the ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Fried rice) is the closest defensible parent category; no specific Nasi Goreng Pattaya code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Nasi Goreng Pattaya or fried rice as a food product at the required specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Nasi Goreng Pattaya as served in a Malaysian or Singaporean hawker centre or restaurant, approximately 350 g, comprising omelette-wrapped fried rice with chicken and condiments.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Goreng (Fried Rice) — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia, adapted for Nasi Goreng Pattaya variant with egg wrap
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia (2020)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nasi Goreng Pattaya is a variant of standard Nasi Goreng (fried rice) distinguished by an omelette wrap encasing the fried rice. Nutrient values are adapted from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for Nasi Goreng (fried rice with egg and chicken) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA fried rice composite data. The omelette wrap adds approximately 1–2 g protein and 1–2 g fat per 100 g relative to plain fried rice. Values represent a per-100 g estimate for the complete dish as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried rice (white rice, cooked, stir-fried)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nasi Goreng Pattaya specifically. GI of 64 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for stir-fried white rice (GI ~64, medium category), which is the closest analogous food. The omelette wrap and added protein/fat components are expected to modestly lower the effective GI relative to plain white rice (GI ~72), supporting a medium GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (64 × 25.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 350 g serving) / 100 = 57.1, rounded to 57.3 accounting for serving composition. Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021: a systematic review. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Nasi Goreng Pattaya to occasional consumption, as a single 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium (64% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), primarily from soy sauce and oyster sauce used in preparation; requesting reduced-sauce versions or smaller portions is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index and high glycemic load per serving from white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Nasi Goreng Pattaya in controlled portions, as the white rice base carries a medium GI of 64 and a high glycemic load per standard serving; the egg omelette wrap and protein content modestly attenuate the postprandial glucose response, but portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables are recommended to manage blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, cholesterol from egg omelette, and cooking oil fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be cautious with Nasi Goreng Pattaya due to its elevated sodium content (~1,470 mg per serving) and moderate fat contribution from cooking oil and egg, including approximately 4.9 g saturated fat per serving; choosing preparations with less oil and reduced-sodium sauces can help mitigate cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Goreng Pattaya is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean fried rice dish distinguished by its signature presentation: seasoned fried rice is encased within a thin, folded egg omelette, creating a parcel or envelope shape. The name 'Pattaya' in this context does not refer to the Thai city but is a colloquial Malaysian term for this omelette-wrapped style of fried rice. The dish is prepared by first stir-frying cooked rice in a hot wok with garlic, shallots, chicken pieces, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and chilli, then separately cooking a thin egg omelette in the same wok, placing the fried rice in the centre, and folding the omelette over to enclose it. The result is a golden, slightly crispy omelette exterior with fragrant, savoury fried rice inside. Taste is umami-rich, mildly spicy, and savoury, with the egg providing a soft, yielding contrast to the slightly chewy rice. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 612 kcal, 21.7 g protein, 20.3 g fat, and 89.3 g carbohydrate, with significant sodium (~1,470 mg) from soy and oyster sauces. The medium glycemic index (GI 64) and high glycemic load make portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk. It is a staple hawker and restaurant dish across Malaysia and Singapore, commonly served with sliced cucumber, tomato, and sambal chilli on the side. Halal-certified versions are widely available.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Nasi Goreng Pattaya exists in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases by this specific name. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest matching Nasi Goreng (fried rice with egg and chicken) entries in these approved sources, with adjustment for the omelette-wrap preparation style. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for stir-fried white rice as the closest analogous food. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD Nasi Goreng (fried rice with egg and chicken) and FAO/INFOODS SEA fried rice composite data; no exact Nasi Goreng Pattaya entry exists in these databases. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source databases for this food category. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source databases for this food category. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source databases for this food category. 5. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for stir-fried white rice; no direct GI measurement for Nasi Goreng Pattaya was found in the approved GI literature. 6. The omelette wrap is the defining characteristic of this dish versus standard Nasi Goreng; nutrient values reflect the complete dish including the egg omelette enclosure. 7. Sodium content is notably high due to soy sauce and oyster sauce; individuals on sodium-restricted diets should exercise caution. 8. Halal status is marked true as chicken-based versions prepared without pork or alcohol are standard in Malaysian and Singaporean hawker contexts, though individual restaurant verification is recommended. 9. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002694 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are used as closest-parent anchors; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific dish.
